evidence: src/integrations/supabase/client.ts keeps three access surfaces simultaneously (`initializeSupabase`, `getSupabaseClient`, and deprecated `supabase` proxy), which preserves legacy behavior but blurs the canonical boundary for data access ownership.

src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx and src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts both keep deprecated compatibility exports (`useMobile` barrel and `reportRuntimeError`/`RuntimeErrorOptions`), extending migration-era API surfaces in highly imported modules.

src/types/generationAndShots.ts and src/shared/lib/utils.ts are broad shared barrels that flatten dependency boundaries; many modules can depend on aggregate surfaces instead of domain-local entry points.

src/pages/Home/components/panes/CreativePartnerPane.tsx combines product narrative content, routing actions, tooltip interaction policy, and mobile behavior in one component, while src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx combines dialog shell concerns with global scroll/viewport workarounds and pane-layout coordination.

Client-boundary access is inconsistent across collaborating data modules: `getSupabaseClient()` is used in `generationTaskRepository.ts` and `generationTaskCache.ts`, while `generationTransformers.ts`, `useLineageChain.ts`, and `generateVideoService.ts` import a module-level `supabase` client directly.

Error/lifecycle handoff is not predictable across adjacent seams: `useLineageChain.ts` logs and returns `[]` on fetch failures, `generationTaskRepository.ts` returns status-tagged mappings (`query_failed`, `scope_mismatch`), and `generateVideoService.ts` converts exceptions to `{ success: false, error }`.

Repository status metadata is dropped at the cache-to-UI seam: `generationTaskCache.ts` stores `{ taskId, status }`, but `enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData` only forwards `taskId/taskData`, discarding failure states needed by downstream consumers.

Rate-limit enforcement seam is fail-open without explicit degraded signal: `checkRateLimit` returns `allowed: true` on RPC failures and catch paths, but the API surface exposes no degradation marker to callers.

findings.summary: Legacy compatibility layers keep multiple architectural entry points alive in core shared modules.
evidence: Supabase client exposes both new runtime API and deprecated proxy export in the same module, including migration metadata and fallback behavior.
Deprecated compatibility exports remain in high-traffic shared modules (`useMobile` barrel and `reportRuntimeError` alias), so consumers can continue bypassing target boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Set a hard deprecation removal sprint: add lint bans/codemods for deprecated imports, migrate callers to canonical APIs, then remove proxy/alias exports so each subsystem has one authoritative entry point.

findings.summary: High-import shared barrels create dependency gravity that weakens domain ownership boundaries.
evidence: `generationAndShots.ts` is a canonical aggregate type barrel, encouraging broad cross-feature coupling through one shared surface.
`shared/lib/utils.ts` and `shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx` re-export mixed helper/UI primitives, letting features import through generic hubs instead of domain-scoped modules.
findings.suggestion: Replace broad barrels with narrower domain entry points (for example per-feature type modules and UI primitive subpaths), then enforce import-path rules to keep dependencies aligned with ownership boundaries.

findings.summary: Key components mix multiple responsibilities, making ownership and decomposition harder to reason about.
evidence: `CreativePartnerPane` bundles marketing copy, navigation, external links, tooltip lifecycle rules, and mobile input branching in one feature component.
`LightboxShell` is nominally a shell but also owns global scroll-lock/viewport repair behavior and tasks-pane layout negotiation.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers` centralizes unrelated gallery actions (navigation, notifier handling, task-details modal orchestration), indicating blurred role boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Split these modules by responsibility: isolate presentation content, interaction policies, and global environment adapters into dedicated collaborators with explicit ownership contracts.

findings.summary: Orchestrator/run ID handoffs use multiple param shapes, forcing fragile fallback queries.
evidence: `payloadBuilder` emits top-level `orchestrator_task_id` and `run_id`, while `joinClips` nests equivalents under `orchestrator_details`.

`orchestrator.ts` must query multiple JSON paths for the same identifiers (`params->>orchestrator_run_id`, `params->>run_id`, `params->orchestrator_details->>run_id` and analogous orchestrator_task_id variants).
Generation routing also reads parent linkage from mixed locations (`params.parent_generation_id` and `params.orchestrator_details.parent_generation_id`).
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical task-linkage contract (single location for run ID, orchestrator ID, parent ID), add a shared adapter used by all task builders, and simplify completion queries to that canonical shape.

findings.summary: Structure-guidance normalization and legacy-key cleanup is duplicated across task builders.
evidence: `payloadBuilder.ts` builds unified `structure_guidance` then deletes a local `legacyStructureParams` list.
`individualTravelSegment.ts` repeats the same cleanup pattern via `LEGACY_STRUCTURE_PARAMS` and additional deletes in orchestrator/task payload builders.

`VideoTravelDetails.tsx` must read structure data from several fallback locations (`individual_segment_params`, `orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, top-level params), indicating boundary translation is not centralized.
findings.suggestion: Extract a single shared task-param canonicalizer for structure guidance (normalize + strip legacy + read helpers) and use it in both task creation and task detail rendering paths.

findings.summary: Several hooks add pass-through wrapper layers without meaningful boundary reduction.
evidence: `useImageEditValue` defines a very wide `UseImageEditValueParams` contract and mostly re-emits fields into another object, creating extra seam surface.
`useButtonGroupProps` performs nearly pure field copying into grouped objects with long dependency lists, but no real translation or policy.
`useInlineEditState` orchestrates many sub-hooks and forwards large state bags into view/components, making integration changes ripple across multiple wrappers.
findings.suggestion: Replace copy-through wrappers with cohesive domain slices (e.g., `inpaintModel`, `variantModel`, `actions`) exposed directly from one controller hook, and remove wrapper hooks that only remap property names.

findings.summary: Join-clips creation keeps dual legacy/new input formats in one boundary, increasing seam ambiguity.
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskParams` supports both `clips[]` and legacy `starting_video_path`/`ending_video_path`/`intermediate_video_paths`.
`buildClipSequence` conditionally constructs a normalized list from either shape, so the seam accepts multiple caller contracts indefinitely.
Additional options (per-join overrides, loop mode, edit mode, audio) are mixed into the same payload builder, increasing boundary condition combinations.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a strict v2 input contract for join creation (single clip-list shape), move legacy mapping to a compatibility adapter at the callsite, and deprecate legacy fields from the core task builder.

findings.summary: Supabase access seam mixes singleton and runtime getter patterns across linked services.
evidence: `generationTaskRepository.ts` and `generationTaskCache.ts` call `getSupabaseClient()`.
`generationTransformers.ts`, `useLineageChain.ts`, and `generateVideoService.ts` import `supabase` directly.
These modules participate in the same generation/task data flow, but their boundary contract for obtaining DB clients is inconsistent.
findings.suggestion: Standardize one data-access seam for this subsystem (prefer injected or getter-based runtime client), then update these services to use that seam consistently so auth lifecycle and test mocking behavior are predictable.

findings.summary: Adjacent generation APIs propagate failures with incompatible contracts.
evidence: `useLineageChain.ts` catches query errors, calls `handleError`, and returns `[]` (same shape as a real empty lineage).
`generationTaskRepository.ts` encodes failures in typed statuses like `query_failed` and `scope_mismatch`.
`generateVideoService.ts` converts errors into `{ success: false, error }` return values.
`toolSettingsService.ts` throws on most failures (except select fallback paths), adding another behavior pattern.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a shared operation-result contract for generation-related seams (for example `{ ok, value, errorCode }`) and avoid returning data-like success values for failure paths; this lets consumers handle retries, UX messaging, and telemetry consistently.

findings.summary: Task-mapping failure status is lost when cache data is transformed for UI use.
evidence: `generationTaskRepository.ts` returns rich mapping statuses (`ok`, `missing_generation`, `scope_mismatch`, `invalid_tasks_shape`, `query_failed`).
`generationTaskCache.ts` stores `{ taskId, status }` in query cache via `taskQueryKeys.generationMapping(...)`.
`enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData` reads cached mapping but only forwards `taskId/taskData`, dropping `status` so downstream layers cannot distinguish missing data from failed lookups.
findings.suggestion: Expose mapping `status` (and optional `queryError`) in the enhanced generation shape or a parallel field, then use it in consuming UI/hooks to branch behavior for retry/error messaging instead of treating all null-task outcomes equally.

findings.summary: Rate-limit helper fail-open path hides infrastructure failure from API callers.
evidence: `checkRateLimit` returns `allowed: true` when RPC fails or throws (both error paths).
The returned contract does not include a degraded-state flag, so callers cannot distinguish legit allowance from rate-limiter outage.
`edgeRequest.ts` provides structured error/response envelope helpers, but the degraded condition is not surfaced through a comparable response contract.
findings.suggestion: Extend `RateLimitResult` with a degradation signal (for example `enforcement: 'active' | 'degraded'`) and propagate it via headers/log context so handlers can emit observability signals and optional defensive policy when limiter infrastructure is unhealthy.

findings.summary: The shared hooks package mixes reusable primitives with domain-specific token management.
evidence: `useApiTokens` performs PAT-specific data access (`user_api_tokens`, `generate-pat`, `revoke-pat`) and auth checks, while neighboring hooks are generic cross-domain primitives.
This makes `src/shared/hooks` ownership ambiguous: both feature logic and reusable infrastructure are treated as the same package boundary.
findings.suggestion: Move `useApiTokens` into a domain folder (for example `src/features/apiTokens/hooks/` or billing/account settings area) and keep `src/shared/hooks` for domain-agnostic hooks only.

findings.summary: LoRA-specific UI and behavior are placed in the global shared components layer.
evidence: `ActiveLoRAsDisplay` embeds LoRA domain rules (trigger word handling, LoRA metadata lookup, LoRA preview media detection) rather than just presentational primitives.
Its colocated test asserts domain actions (`onAddTriggerWord`, LoRA strength semantics), confirming this is feature-level behavior rather than generic shared UI.
findings.suggestion: Relocate this component and test into the feature/tool that owns LoRA workflows, and keep only reusable UI primitives in `src/shared/components`.

findings.summary: Test directory conventions are inconsistent across nearby shared modules.
evidence: Hook tests are grouped in `__tests__` under `src/shared/hooks`, but component tests in this batch are colocated in `src/shared/components` root.
Mixed placement patterns in the same shared area weakens predictability of where tests and ownership boundaries live.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one test placement convention per top-level package (either consistent colocated tests or consistent `__tests__` folders) and migrate these files to match.

findings.summary: Removal/deprecation contracts are comments only, not enforceable quality gates.
evidence: `README.md` lists an enforced gate for `@/types/shots` migration, but no equivalent enforcement is declared for Supabase or error-handling deprecations.
`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` defines `LEGACY_SUPABASE_PROXY_REMOVE_BY` and `@removalTarget 2026-06-30` only as metadata/comments.
`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` keeps deprecated aliases (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) with removal tags but no runtime/build-time guard.
findings.suggestion: Add explicit CI checks (lint/script) for deprecated import/export usage tied to each removal target and fail builds when deadlines pass; keep one shared deprecation registry rather than per-file comments.

findings.summary: Supabase bootstrap contract is undermined by permissive fallback initialization.
evidence: `README.md` frames bootstrap as explicit (`initializeSupabase()` called once), indicating a clear architecture boundary.
`getSupabaseClient()` catches runtime-access failures and initializes lazily as fallback, allowing call sites to bypass bootstrap ordering without immediate failure.
Legacy `supabase` proxy remains exported, keeping pre-contract usage patterns alive while runtime still recovers automatically.
findings.suggestion: Make fallback behavior environment-gated (e.g., tests/dev only) and fail fast in production when bootstrap order is violated; phase out proxy usage behind a hard migration deadline with automated detection.

findings.summary: Compatibility policy is fragmented across modules instead of one coherent contract layer.
evidence: `README.md` declares `generationAndShots` as canonical and advertises strict migration governance for that path.
Other critical compatibility surfaces (`supabase` proxy and error alias exports) use local, module-specific deprecation annotations and owners, creating parallel governance styles.
No single compatibility entrypoint or policy module is referenced from these files, so contract discoverability depends on scanning individual files.
findings.suggestion: Create a centralized compatibility-policy module/doc that lists active shims, owners, deadlines, and enforcement command(s), and reference it from README plus each shim module.
